scenarios.name: Breach of Contract — Damages (Business)

checks.detail: Total claim Rs.32L or breakdown stated

scenarios.draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE COMMERCIAL COURT (COMMERCIAL DIVISION)
AT BENGALURU

C.S. No. __________ / 20____

BETWEEN:

TechSoft Solutions Pvt Ltd,
A Company registered under the Companies Act, 2013,
Having its registered office at MG Road, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Director Priya Menon,
                                                    ... PLAINTIFF

AND:

CloudBridge Systems LLP,
A Limited Liability Partnership registered under the LLP Act, 2008,
Having its registered office at Whitefield, Bengaluru,
Represented by its Designated Partner Vikram Singh,
                                                    ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF DAMAGES FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT UNDER SECTION 73 OF THE INDIAN CONTRACT ACT, 1872

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

COMMERCIAL COURT MAINTAINABILITY

1. The subject matter of the present suit arises out of a commercial dispute within the meaning of Section 2(1)(c) of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015. The suit value exceeds the specified value as prescribed under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015, and is therefore maintainable before this Hon'ble Commercial Court.

JURISDICTION

2. The Plaintiff is a company registered under the Companies Act, 2013 and is sui juris, competent to institute this suit. The Defendant is a limited liability partnership registered under the LLP Act, 2008 and is sui juris, capable of being sued.

3. The contract was executed in Bengaluru and was to be performed in Bengaluru. The Defendant resides and works for profit at Whitefield, Bengaluru. Thus, this Hon'ble Court has jurisdiction under Section 20 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The suit is filed in the Court of the lowest grade competent to try it under Section 15 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908. The dispute qualifies as a commercial dispute of specified value under the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

SECTION 12A COMPLIANCE

4. The Plaintiff states that {{MEDIATION_COMPLIANCE_STATUS - Select: (a) Pre-institution mediation was conducted on [DATE] before [MEDlATION CENTRE] and concluded without settlement, Certificate enclosed; OR (b) Urgent interim relief is being sought, hence pre-institution mediation is dispensed with under Section 12A proviso}}. This suit complies with Section 12A of the Commercial Courts Act, 2015.

LIMITATION

5. The suit is within limitation under Article 55 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, the period prescribed being three years from the date on which the contract was broken. The cause of action arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project. The suit is filed well within the prescribed three-year period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

6. On 01.02.2024, the Plaintiff and Defendant entered into a written Software Development Agreement for development of an ERP system at Bengaluru, as per Annexure A.

7. The total contract value was Rs. 45,00,000/-. The Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000/- as first milestone payment via NEFT (UTR: ICIC20240201456789) on 01.02.2024, as per Annexure B.

8. The Defendant was bound to deliver the first module by 01.05.2024. Both parties are competent to contract, being registered entities under applicable laws.

9. The Plaintiff has performed its obligation by making the advance payment and remains ready to perform further obligations.

BREACH OF CONTRACT

10. The Defendant failed to deliver the first module by the agreed deadline of 01.05.2024.

11. On 15.06.2024, the Defendant abandoned the project without delivering any module or returning the advance payment, as evidenced by email correspondence at Annexure C.

12. The Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 (Annexure E) demanding performance and refund. The Defendant has failed to comply to the said notice.

LOSS AND DAMAGE

13. The Plaintiff claims Rs. 20,00,000/- as refund of advance payment, supported by Annexure B.

14. The Plaintiff claims Rs. 12,00,000/- as consequential damages due to delay in operations, supported by loss assessment report at Annexure D.

15. These losses naturally arose from the breach and were within the contemplation of parties at contract formation under the two-limb test of Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

16. The Plaintiff took steps to mitigate loss as detailed in the loss assessment report. There is no claim for compound interest as the contract does not provide for it.

LEGAL BASIS

17. The Plaintiff is entitled to damages under Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 for breach of contract.

18. Time was of the essence for the delivery deadline, invoking Section 55 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

19. No subsequent agreement has novated or discharged the original contract.

20. The Plaintiff has taken steps to minimize loss, pre-empting any defence regarding failure to mitigate.

CAUSE OF ACTION

21. The cause of action first arose on 15.06.2024 when the Defendant abandoned the project and failed to deliver the module.

22. The suit is filed within three years from the date of breach as per Article 55 of the Limitation Act, 1963.

23. The breach constitutes a single event of abandonment and non-performance, not a continuing breach.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

24. The total suit valuation is Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only) for purposes of jurisdiction and court fee.

25. The court fee paid is {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}} as per the {{COURT_FEE_ACT_NAME}} applicable in Karnataka.

26. The plaint is properly stamped under the applicable Court Fee legislation.

PRAYER

27. In the premises aforesaid, the Plaintiff most respectfully prays that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:
(a) Award damages in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for Rs. 32,00,000/- (Rupees Thirty-Two Lakhs Only);
(b) Award pendente lite interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from the date of institution of the suit till decree under Order XX Rule 11 CPC;
(c) Award future interest at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems fit from the date of decree till realisation under Section 34 CPC;
(d) Award costs of the suit including advocate's fees to the Plaintiff;
(e) Grant such other and further relief(s) as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper.

SCHEDULE OF DAMAGES / PARTICULARS OF DAMAGES

The Plaintiff claims the following heads of damages with computation methodology:

(i) Refund of Advance Payment
     Amount claimed: Rs. 20,00,000/-
     Basis: First milestone payment under Software Development Agreement
     Documentary proof: Annexure B (NEFT receipt UTR: ICIC20240201456789)

(ii) Consequential Damages
     Amount claimed: Rs. 12,00,000/-
     Basis: Loss assessment report by auditor for operational delay
     Documentary proof: Annexure D

TOTAL DAMAGES CLAIMED: Rs. 32,00,000/-

LIST OF DOCUMENTS

Annexure A — Software Development Agreement dated 01.02.2024

Annexure B — NEFT receipt (UTR: ICIC20240201456789)

Annexure C — Email correspondence showing abandonment

Annexure D — Loss assessment report by auditor

Annexure E — Legal notice dated 01.07.2024

VERIFICATION

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the authorised representative of the Plaintiff above-named, do hereby verify that the contents of paragraphs 1 to 27 of this plaint are true and correct to my knowledge, and no material facts have been concealed therefrom, and that I have not filed any other suit, petition, or proceeding in any court or before any authority in respect of the same subject matter and cause of action.

DEPONENT

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

STATEMENT OF TRUTH

I, {{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, the Plaintiff through its authorised representative herein, do hereby state on solemn affirmation that the contents of the above plaint and the facts stated in paragraphs 1 to 27 are true to my knowledge and belief and nothing material has been concealed therefrom.

I understand that I shall be liable to proceedings under law for making a false statement.

Verified at {{CITY}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.
